name: New Student & Family Programs
summary: Begin your journey at TCU by attending Orientation, Frog Camp, and Frogs First all before your first semester. Continue your development by participating in First-Year Leadership Opportunities (FLOs) and Introduction to University Life (UNLF).

name: TCU Programs, Departments, and Classes
summary: This TCU Engage section is for any TCU program, department or class in order to provide engagement, involvement and leadership opportunities.

name: Student Filmmakers Association
summary: SFA is a student organization that produces multiple short films each semester and provides those involved professional, on-set experiences. SFA meets every Tuesday at 5:00pm in Moudy South 164!

name: Society of Physics Students
summary: TCU students who appreciate physics.

name: National Student Speech Language Hearing Association
summary: Local organization of the National Speech-Language and Hearing Association, which helps students connect to national office for journals, websites, and interpersonal connection with other Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists.

name: TCU Finance Club
summary:
The purpose of Finance Club shall be to:
1. Assist in the professional, educational, & social development of all students interested in the Finance Industry. 
2. Provide an association for students actively interested in careers in the Finance Industry.

name: TCU Collegiate Chapter of MTNA (Music Teachers National Association)
summary: The purpose of this organization is to advance the value and study of music making to society and to support the professionalism of music teachers.  We will also organize community service projects to share the joy of music making with others.

name: MBA Marketing Association
summary: The Neeley MBA Marketing Association's mission is to foster academic and professional development through the coordination of networking opportunities, educational events and company interactions that equip our members with the tools necessary to becom..

name: BNSF Neeley Leadership Program
summary:
Leaders at work. Leaders in life.

BNSF Neeley Leadership Program (NLP): We prepare leaders who enable people and organizations to meet the needs of an ever-changing, complex world.

name: Association of Latino Professionals For America
summary: Alpfa's mission is to:To empower and develop Latino men and women as leaders of character for the nation, in every sector of the global economy.Furthermore, there is a real need for more Latino leaders. At a time when the business community desperately...

name: Women in Science and Engineering
summary: The Women in Science and Engineering organization seeks to establish a network between female science majors and provide the needed resources for members to succeed in college & beyond. Check out our Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/TCUwomeninscience..

name: TCU Tunnel of Expression
summary: Tunnel of Expression is an interactive event that highlights contemporary issues of oppression.  It is designed to introduce participants to concepts of oppression, privilege, and power.

name: American Marketing Association
summary: TCU's American Marketing Association empowers students of all majors with experiential learning and industry engagements to secure competitive internships, launch high-paying careers, and drive business success.

name: Gamer's Guild
summary: Provides and encourages a social outlet for everyone in all forms of gaming. There are no dues and no mandatory meetings. Come; just have fun!

name: Capital Markets and Corporate Finance Club.
summary: The TCU Capital Markets Club is a recognized student organization for graduate business students at Neeley School of Business who are interested in corporate finance, investments, private equity, real estate, venture capital, and alternative investments.

name: Physician Assistant Club
summary: Physician Assistant Club promotes student awareness and interest in the occupation of a Physician Assistant. We are an organization designed to provide resources for those interested in becoming a physician assistant.

name: Horn Society
summary: The purpose of this organization is to promote the TCU Horn Program within the School of Music, encourage students to interact and cooperate with other musicians, and to continuously strive to improve their musical goals.

name: Music Educators Organizations
summary: The Music Educators Organization aims to provide opportunities for networking, professional development, and service to students interested in the field of music education!

name: Real Estate Club
summary: This organization was created to empower students with the knowledge, connections, and experiences needed to thrive in the world of real estate.

name: TCU Math Club
summary: The TCU Math Club hopes to foster a greater understanding and appreciation of mathematics, to encourage undergraduate activity in math research and mathematical experiences, and to provide a social & intellectual forum for all students.

name: Student Association of Social Work (SASW)
summary: SASW's mission is to connect all students with the professional organization through service, advocacy, and education.

name: Society of Women Engineers
summary: Society of Women Engineers, or SWE, is an organization aimed for all engineering majors at TCU. The goal of TCU SWE is to allow our members to participate in networking opportunities with local companies and gain resources in their career development.

name: Women's Business Network
summary: Women's Business Network seeks to empower young women in Neeley to lead and achieve by developing their potential through networking, mentoring, and community service.

name: Society of Composers
summary: This is a forum for student composers to get together and present their music and to learn about music of others, including new compositional techniques and styles. We host masterclasses, guest composers and performers. We welcome anyone interested!

name: Asian Media Association
summary: TCU's Asian Media Association strives to create an environment in which people of common interests can come to appreciate media from Asia and make friends. We are an easygoing club open to all, with no dues and no mandatory meetings.

name: Computer Science Society
summary: The Computer Science Society provides opportunities for community and mentorship within the Computer Science field.

name: Psi Chi
summary: International Honor Society of Psychology

name: Phi Upsilon Omicron
summary: Phi Upsilon Omicron is a national honor society for the integrated fields of family and consumer sciences and was founded on the University of Minnesota campus in 1909. The TCU Beta Zeta Chapter was chartered in 1968.

name: Geography Club
summary: Founded alongside the Geography Department, the TCU Geography Club seeks to promote the study of geography among students and serves to connect geographers on and off campus. If you don't know much about university-level geography this org is for you!

name: Trombone Society
summary: The purpose of the organization is to promote communications among TCU area trombonists; to improve the artistic level of performance, teaching and literature associated with the trombone family; and to function as a local clearing house for trombone r...

name: Economics Club
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide a resource for Economics majors and minors to meet one another, to discuss current economic issues, to facilitate events related to the study of economics, and to be an academic and professional resource.

name: theCrew
summary: TheCrew is an organization made up of students with the sole purpose of brightening other people's days! Each event we plan is facilitated by our members. If you want to bring bounce houses, petting zoos, or anything else to campus, join theCrew!

name: License to Trill
summary: License to Trill is TCU's gender-inclusive a cappella group, founded in 2015. Our mission is to share a cappella music with the TCU Campus and Fort Worth area while building community through our shared love of music!

name: Public Relations Student Society of America
summary: We advocate rigorous academic standards for public relations education, the highest ethical principles and diversity in the profession.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
summary: Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) is a cultural student organization that strives to strengthen student unity, represent Vietnamese students’ voices, and enhance diversity at TCU. We are passionate about promoting Vietnamese culture at TCU through...

name: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Incorporated (Eta Psi Chapter)
summary: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Incorporated is a brotherhood of gentlemen who share the common goal of upholding the aims of the fraternity: Manly Deeds, Scholarship, and Love for ALL Mankind. This fraternity is deeply rooted in the community and particip...

name: Graduate Student Senate
summary: The GSS is the official representative body for graduate students at TCU and provides a forum for discussing issues, coordinating efforts, and promoting graduate programs with the administration.

name: Geological Society
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to promote geologic training and community building.

name: Chi Alpha
summary: We are a community of students that desires to follow Christ together and serve our campus, community, and world. We meet weekly in LifeGroups, and have tons of other opportunities to grow spiritually, connect with friends, and to help others in need...

name: Design Focus
summary: TCU Graphic Design Organization

name: Robotics and Artificial Intelligence Club
summary: To create a community on campus that promotes the advancement and practice of robotics, artificial intelligence, and any fields of arts or sciences related therein.

name: Interior Design Campus Center
summary: To promote high ethical and design standards, to benefit the membership of the organization by providing an exchange of ideas, a common meeting ground for understanding, cooperation and education and an opportunity to keep abreast of development.

name: Community Scholars
summary: The Community Scholars Program recruits top candidates from urban, high minority public high schools and offers more than $6 million in scholarships each year.

name: Gamma Iota Sigma: International Risk Management, Insurance and Actuarial Science Collegiate Fraternity
summary: GIS pursues a mission to promote and encourage student interest in the industry as a profession while encouraging the high moral and scholastic attainments of its members.

name: TCU Volunteer Opportunities
summary: Come do service with us! We work hard to provide YOU meaningful service opportunities in our Fort Worth community and beyond. Check out our events to make an impact because Frogs Serve!

name: Lead NOW (Network of Women)
summary: Lead NOW (Network of Women) is a first-year women's leadership organization. Prior application and admission is required!

name: Chancellor's Leadership Program
summary: TCU Engage page for the Chancellor's Leadership Program, a 2.5 year cohort based leadership program at TCU.

name: Criminal Justice Society
summary: The Criminal Justice Society is an inclusive club for Criminal Justice majors and minors. We provide educational, volunteer, and networking opportunities to aid students in expanding their knowledge of the professional field of Criminal Justice.

name: Society for Advancement of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans in Science
summary: The purpose of TCU SACNAS is to offer a powerful and inclusive source of community that provides local and regional support for college students and professionals to succeed in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM).

name: TCU Student Organization Presidents
summary: This is a page for all current TCU student organization presidents to find information for their role, events for learning, group updates and recognition/renewal timelines. You can also communicate and share opportunities with other presidents here.

name: Graduate Supply Chain Club
summary: Our mission is to provide our members with a better understanding of the Supply Chain field through educational programming, firsthand experience with local industry, networking opportunities with alumni and experienced professionals.

name: Turning Point
summary: Turning Point's purpose is to educate students about the importance of fiscal responsibility, limited government, and free markets. We hope to identify students who believe in free speech and individual liberty.

name: Theatre TCU Black Excellence
summary: The purpose of this organization is to educate, uplift, and provide a safe space for Black students within the College of Fine Arts to create art and to educate themselves and others about black artists throughout history.

name: Biology Club at TCU
summary: We provide an outlet for students to enjoy and develop a greater appreciation for Biology and related areas. Our club events include social events, volunteer opportunities, and panel events with professionals in the field.

name: Patrick's Purpose to Value Life
summary: Our goal is to create an authentic sense of awareness and cultivate action to prevent adolescent suicides by bringing light to a tragedy that hits close to home for many TCU students. We will work endlessly to save valuable and worthy lives.

name: Neeley Office Of Inclusive Excellence
summary: The TCU Neeley Office of Inclusive Excellence is a physical space for ALL Horned Frogs to gather & connect across differences. The office is focused on creating and supporting a community that welcomes and champions diverse cultures and identities.

name: Women in Computer Science
summary: The purpose of the Women in Computer Science organization is to support and empower female and non-binary undergraduate students in the Computer Science field by building a social, technical, and networking community for its members.

name: Nontraditional Frogs
summary: Nontraditional Frogs exist for the purpose of creating a supportive community for nontraditional students.

name: Bilingual Education Student Organization at TCU
summary: The Bilingual Education Student Organization is dedicated to creating an inclusive and supportive community for all bilingual and multilingual students, regardless of language background or proficiency level.

name: Cultural Connectors
summary: TCU Cultural Connectors are student leaders in our residence halls who coordinate programs around Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion #Frogs4theCulture

name: TCU Library
summary: A place for research, studying, community lectures, library workshops, and events.

name: TCU LGBTQ+ Resources
summary: TCU Engage page for LGBTQ+ Resources.

name: Center for Supply Chain Innovation
summary: Engage page for the Center for Supply Chain Innovation within the Neeley School of Business

name: CHXX Who Lift
summary: CHXX Who Lift empowers women in the weight room, to learn more about weight lifting, and to foster a positive mental environment where women can gather to discuss difficult topics such as mental health, body image, etc.

name: Management Club
summary: The purpose of the Management Club is to provide events, networking opportunities, and resources for current and interested Neeley students interested in a career in management.

name: Minorities Opening Roads in Engineering
summary: The purpose of M.O.R.E. is to challenge stereotypes in STEM and empower students in their college journey while giving back to the underrepresented communities these students came from.

name: Scribble Frogs
summary: As TCU's creative writing club, Scribble Frogs strives to create a safe and collaborative space for student writers. During our weekly meetings, we have guest speakers, writing workshops, literary games, and more! Come to our meetings to learn more!

name: International Services
summary: International Services works to build innovative and tangible programs to engage international and domestic students towards TCU mission of creating responsible, ethical global leaders.

name: Rural, International, & Global Health
summary: The RIG SIG seeks to address issues regarding healthcare disparities globally and rurally while also uniting, educating, and inspiring students to pursue health equity.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
summary: The purpose of this organization is to empower the Hispanic community to realize its fullest potential and to impact the world through STEM awareness, access, support, and development.

name: Leadership and Academics for STEM Students Organization
summary: This organization would serve as a community and network building tool for STEM students. We focus on engagement with other students in similar fields of study, while providing mentorship and opportunities for service.

name: Family Medicine Interest Group
summary: The Family Medicine Interest Group (FMIG) at Burnett School of Medicine aims to explore the field of family medicine, make a meaningful impact in our community, and build lasting connections with peers and mentors passionate about holistic patient care.

name: Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation Interest Group
summary: Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation Interest Group

name: Oncology Interest Group
summary: Oncology Interest Group is all about connecting students who are passionate about oncology. Whether it's through engaging seminars, research projects, or just chatting with leading oncologists, we're here to learn, share, and make a difference together.

name: Orthopedic Surgery Interest Group
summary: The Orthopedic Student Interest Group is designed for those interested in learning more about the field and individuals who are pursuing orthopedic surgery as a career in medicine.

name: TCU Postcolonial Scholars Society
summary: A student organization that engages with political and cultural topics about empires, colonialism, and globalization and seeks to prepare TCU students better to be ethical leaders and global citizens.

name: Business Information Systems Club
summary: This club is dedicated to showing BIS majors different career paths and preparing them to enter the professional world. Meetings will consist of case study practice, alumni speakers, and recruiters giving tips and tricks for the business realm.

name: MY Harris - Student Resource Group
summary: MY Harris is a resource for Harris College students who identify as members of underrepresented groups. It's a place to gain strategies for navigating TCU and other spaces.

name: Letters of Love
summary: Letters of Love is a global non-profit organization that provides emotional support for patients in children's hospitals worldwide through handmade cards. Join us and spread love.

name: Association for Computing Machinery at (ACM TCU)
summary: Building up & supporting students interested in tech regardless of majors/backgrounds

name: English Graduate Student Association
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide opportunities for for English graduate students to grow as academics, find community among like-minded scholars, provide mentorship opportunities, and give space for graduate students.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi - Pre-Law Society
summary: The purpose of the Chapter Bylaws of the Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Fraternity University of Tennessee, Knoxville Chapter is to regulate aspects of the chapter which are subject to change on a semester to semester basis.

name: Young Americans for Freedom Chapter
summary: Our purpose is to defend Conservative principles at TCU. We stand for individual freedom, free enterprise, a strong national defense, and traditional American values.

name: Frogs Aiding Immigrants and Refugees
summary: Our mission is to support students in the Fort Worth area who are immigrants and refugees, and to spread awareness about refugee and immigrant communities

name: Nuestra Herencia
summary: Nuestra Herencia will host activities tailored to Spanish heritage speakers, including conversation hours, movie nights, guest lecturers, and working with members of Career Services to guide students on the process of writing a resume, etc.

name: Ruth Jackson Orthopaedic Society
summary: N/A

name: Med Mentorship SIG
summary: n/a

name: Research Journal Club
summary: n/a

name: Leaders Engaging & Assisting People through Service (LEAPS)
summary: Leaders Engaging & Assisting People through Service (LEAPS) is a First-Year Leadership Organization (FLO) that focuses on community engagement and servant leadership, while also developing and executing TCU Serves Days, etc.

name: Black Graduate Business Association
summary: The mission of the BGBA is to foster an inclusive community for Black graduate students in business, promote professional development, and advocate for diversity and equity within the business school. The BGBA aims to connect students, mentors, alumni.

name: Ascend
summary: Our purpose is to continuously uplift the various cultures across Pan Asia within the context of business through the utilization of the Ascend name and prestige.

name: Spirit and Traditions Advisory Council
summary: A student-led, departmentally supported program dedicated to enhancing Spirit and Traditions at TCU.

name: Society of Automotive Engineers
summary: Society of automotive engineers

name: TCU Global Ambassadors
summary: TCU Global Ambassadors

name: ASL Engagement
summary: ASL Engagement

name: Transaction and Investment Professionals Board
summary: TIP Board aims to build a successful and sustainable organization through the development and mentorship of students, giving them the resources and support to develop into confident and inspiring professionals.

name: Frog PATH
summary: Frog PATH is a First-Year Leadership Opportunity (FLO) that provides a community for first-year students passionate about identity, connection, and transformational growth.

name: Center for Writing
summary: The William L. Adams Center for Writing at TCU provides comprehensive writing support for students at all stages of the writing process. We offer a wide range of services, including in-person and Zoom appointments, online submissions, and writing groups.

name: Nationally Competitive Scholarships
summary: Nationally Competitive Scholarships (NCS) helps TCU students discover and apply for prestigious, external awards to study, research, and lead around the world.

name: Investment Club
summary: To help students gain a deeper understanding of investments, financial markets, and personal finance to help make informed decisions about their futures.

name: Theta Tau
summary: Theta Tau Psi is a professional engineering fraternity at TCU focused on community, leadership, and academic and professional development. We bring engineers together through service, mentorship, and meaningful engagement.

name: Bridgepoint
summary: Connecting TCU alumni to current students.

name: The Hippocratic Society at TCU
summary: The Hippocratic Society exists to bridge the gap between science and humanity in pre-health education.

name: Latin Graduate Business Network
summary: Latin Graduate Leadership Network
